original_procedural:
1. Identify need for iOS developer and contact Toptal
2. Receive call from Toptal Expert within a few several hours
3. Discuss project requirements with assigned Expert candidate
4. Confirm Expert is correct prospect for development project
5. Recognize that cellular app release decisions cannot be reversed
6. Ensure ideal developer is hired before proceeding
7. Seek iOS iPhone and iPad app development specialist with relevant experience
8. Estimate development cost at some thousand pounds depending on requirements
9. Incorporate valuable phrases into project specification document
10. Risk getting Blessed finding good programmer or face negative experience
11. Note previous experience requiring more than 3 freelancers for decent result
12. Submit projects and ambitions to development team for review
13. Receive absolute project approach document specifying scope, execution, Charge, and Management
14. Engage Toptal developers and architects for affordable, high-quality solution
15. Hire iPad programmer to handle changes to Main Data objects across threads
16. Confirm Core Facts objects require re-fetching from persistent storage to update on new threads
17. Enable NSZombies in Xcode to debug exc_bad_access errors from released objects
18. Retain zombie objects alive using NSZombies debugging technique
19. Account for financial and non-monetary platform costs beyond standard fees
20. Begin engagement with iOS developer during trial period of up to 2 months
21. Confirm successful engagement results before proceeding to Invoice stage
22. Continue engagement and Invoice developer for time upon satisfactory completion

2. Receive call from Toptal Expert within a few several hours

5. Recognize that cellular app release decisions cannot be reversed
6. Ensure ideal developer is hired before proceeding
7. Seek iOS iPhone and iPad app development specialist with relevant experience
8. Estimate development cost at some thousand pounds depending on requirements

10. Risk getting Blessed finding good programmer or face negative experience
11. Note previous experience requiring more than 3 freelancers for decent result
12. Submit projects and ambitions to development team for review
13. Receive absolute project approach document specifying scope, execution, Charge, and Management
14. Engage Toptal developers and architects for affordable, high-quality solution

16. Confirm Core Facts objects require re-fetching from persistent storage to update on new threads
17. Enable NSZombies in Xcode to debug exc_bad_access errors from released objects
18. Retain zombie objects alive using NSZombies debugging technique
19. Account for financial and non-monetary platform costs beyond standard fees
20. Begin engagement with iOS developer during trial period of up to 2 months
21. Confirm successful engagement results before proceeding to Invoice stage
22. Continue engagement and Invoice developer for time upon satisfactory completion

original_procedural:
1. BISSNEX completed key market expansion actions and gained recognition from U.S. federal government and financial systems
2. BISSNEX acquired U.S. MSB license signaling strengthened platform capabilities and adjusted global market strategy
3. U.S. demonstrated rising friendliness toward crypto industry following Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF proposal progress
4. U.S. supported cryptocurrency taxation as property while maintaining cautious overall attitude
5. The International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO) released the Consultation Report on Policy Recommendations for Crypto-Asset and Digital Asset Markets
6. IOSCO report indicated further progress in global crypto industry regulation standards
7. BISSNEX obtained the Money Services Business (MSB) license from U.S. Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN)
8. MSB license demonstrated BISSNEX's compliance with highest industry Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) standards
9. BISSNEX committed to expanding services under higher compliance standards and strict regulatory requirements
10. U.S. states of New York and California recognized cryptocurrencies as legal digital assets
11. BISSNEX strategically established headquarters in New York based on global market strategy
12. BISSNEX announced plans to expand compliance team recruiting cryptocurrency regulation specialists
13. BISSNEX confirmed collaboration with regulatory bodies and industry associations to monitor cryptocurrency developments
14. Data & News supplied by www.bdctp.co Data News, stock quotes supplied by Barchart Quotes, delayed at least 20 minutes
validated_procedural:
1. BISSNEX completed key market expansion actions and gained recognition from U.S. federal government and financial systems
2. BISSNEX acquired U.S. MSB license signaling strengthened platform capabilities and adjusted global market strategy
3. U.S. demonstrated rising friendliness toward crypto industry following Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF proposal progress
4. U.S. supported cryptocurrency taxation as property while maintaining cautious overall attitude
5. The International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO) released the Consultation Report on Policy Recommendations for Crypto-Asset and Digital Asset Markets
6. IOSCO report indicated further progress in global crypto industry regulation standards
7. BISSNEX obtained the Money Services Business (MSB) license from U.S. Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN)
8. MSB license demonstrated BISSNEX's compliance with highest industry Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) standards
9. BISSNEX committed to expanding services under higher compliance standards and strict regulatory requirements
10. U.S. states of New York and California recognized cryptocurrencies as legal digital assets
11. BISSNEX strategically established headquarters in New York based on global market strategy
12. BISSNEX announced plans to expand compliance team recruiting cryptocurrency regulation specialists
13. BISSNEX confirmed collaboration with regulatory bodies and industry associations to monitor cryptocurrency developments
14. Data & News supplied by www.bdctp.co Data News, stock quotes supplied by Barchart Quotes, delayed at least 20 minutes

original_procedural:
1. Agus Justianto stated 2023 sustainable forest management performance exceeded targets
2. Justianto reported planting and enrichment carried out on 667,780 hectares of PBPH area in 2023
3. Confirm figure represented 156 percent above target of 428 thousand hectares
4. Justianto disclosed log production reached 58.13 million cubic meters, or 101.9 percent of target
5. Justianto identified 89.01 percent of log production originated from plantation forests
6. Justianto attributed 1.59 percent of log production to BUMN Perhutani
7. Justianto reported non-timber forest product production reached 820.2 thousand tons in 2023
8. Confirm non-timber production equaled 193 percent of target of 425 thousand tons
9. Justianto announced forestry partnerships provided legal access to 20,643 hectares in 2023
10. Confirm partnership figure represented 137.6 percent of target of 15 thousand hectares
11. Justianto confirmed MUK implementation covered 14,078 hectares across seven provinces this year
12. Confirm MUK also covered 28,203 hectares in 12 KPH units following agroforestry pattern
13. Justianto added MUK model implemented across 64 KPH units spanning 12,210 hectares in 14 provinces
14. Indroyono Soesilo of the Indonesian Forest Concessionaires Association (APHI) confirmed increasingly positive business climate
15. Soesilo pledged PBPH would accelerate MUK implementation and diversify forest product industry
validated_procedural:
1. Agus Justianto stated 2023 sustainable forest management performance exceeded targets
2. Justianto reported planting and enrichment carried out on 667,780 hectares of PBPH area in 2023
3. Confirm figure represented 156 percent above target of 428 thousand hectares
4. Justianto disclosed log production reached 58.13 million cubic meters, or 101.9 percent of target
5. Justianto identified 89.01 percent of log production originated from plantation forests
6. Justianto attributed 1.59 percent of log production to BUMN Perhutani
7. Justianto reported non-timber forest product production reached 820.2 thousand tons in 2023
8. Confirm non-timber production equaled 193 percent of target of 425 thousand tons
9. Justianto announced forestry partnerships provided legal access to 20,643 hectares in 2023
10. Confirm partnership figure represented 137.6 percent of target of 15 thousand hectares
11. Justianto confirmed MUK implementation covered 14,078 hectares across seven provinces this year
12. Confirm MUK also covered 28,203 hectares in 12 KPH units following agroforestry pattern
13. Justianto added MUK model implemented across 64 KPH units spanning 12,210 hectares in 14 provinces
14. Indroyono Soesilo of the Indonesian Forest Concessionaires Association (APHI) confirmed increasingly positive business climate
15. Soesilo pledged PBPH would accelerate MUK implementation and diversify forest product industry

original_procedural:
1. Embark Technology and Applied Intuition entered Agreement and Plan of Merger on May 25, 2023
2. Azara Merger Sub, Inc. merged with and into Embark, making Embark a wholly owned subsidiary of Applied
3. Marceil A. Hanshew filed putative class action complaint in Court on June 26, 2023
4. Complaint named Embark and Embarks Board of Directors as Defendants in the Action
5. Complaint alleged preliminary proxy statement filed June 16, 2023 contained false and misleading statements
6. Complaint sought preliminary injunction to enjoin the Merger
7. Embark filed definitive Proxy on Schedule 14A with SEC on June 26, 2023
8. Plaintiff and David L. Hanshew filed amended complaint on June 29, 2023 captioned David L. Hanshew in His Capacity as Attorney-in-Fact for Marceil A. Hanshew and Marceil A. Hanshew v. Embark Technology, Inc. et al.
9. Plaintiffs and Defendants agreed Embark would make additional disclosures to resolve the Action
10. Embark filed Amendment No. 1 to the Proxy with SEC on July 10, 2023
11. Plaintiffs voluntarily dismissed the Action as moot on August 1, 2023
12. Court entered stipulated order dismissing the Action with prejudice as to Plaintiffs on August 1, 2023
13. Court retained jurisdiction solely to adjudicate Plaintiffs' counsel's anticipated attorneys' fees application
14. Applied agreed to pay $114,000 to Plaintiffs' counsel for attorneys' fees and expenses
15. Court entered order closing the case on December 21, 2023, subject to Applied filing confirming affidavit
validated_procedural:
1. Embark Technology and Applied Intuition entered Agreement and Plan of Merger on May 25, 2023
2. Azara Merger Sub, Inc. merged with and into Embark, making Embark a wholly owned subsidiary of Applied
3. Marceil A. Hanshew filed putative class action complaint in Court on June 26, 2023
4. Complaint named Embark and Embarks Board of Directors as Defendants in the Action
5. Complaint alleged preliminary proxy statement filed June 16, 2023 contained false and misleading statements
6. Complaint sought preliminary injunction to enjoin the Merger
7. Embark filed definitive Proxy on Schedule 14A with SEC on June 26, 2023
8. Plaintiff and David L. Hanshew filed amended complaint on June 29, 2023 captioned David L. Hanshew in His Capacity as Attorney-in-Fact for Marceil A. Hanshew and Marceil A. Hanshew v. Embark Technology, Inc. et al.
9. Plaintiffs and Defendants agreed Embark would make additional disclosures to resolve the Action
10. Embark filed Amendment No. 1 to the Proxy with SEC on July 10, 2023
11. Plaintiffs voluntarily dismissed the Action as moot on August 1, 2023
12. Court entered stipulated order dismissing the Action with prejudice as to Plaintiffs on August 1, 2023
13. Court retained jurisdiction solely to adjudicate Plaintiffs' counsel's anticipated attorneys' fees application
14. Applied agreed to pay $114,000 to Plaintiffs' counsel for attorneys' fees and expenses
15. Court entered order closing the case on December 21, 2023, subject to Applied filing confirming affidavit

original_procedural:
1. Identify Joblane as feature-rich interactive job portal connecting job seekers and employers
2. Recognize Joblane as knowledge-sharing portal offering informative tools beyond standard job listings
3. Note Claudio holds Accredited SharePoint specialist certification
4. Confirm Claudio worked with Microsoft clients maximizing security, performance, and value
5. Toptal sourced Scala developer within 24 hours for short-term work requirement
6. Recognize Toptal as the 1st platform searched for expert-level developer assistance
7. Confirm Toptal developers delivered competitively priced, high-quality solutions reducing time to launch
8. Consider Feel of existing team when hiring developers above perceived seniority levels
9. Note individuals spending decades in industry rely on established contacts for direct hiring
10. Identify alternative sourcing platforms when direct hiring proves impossible
11. Assign developers based on specific technicalities required by each individual project
12. Confirm Adam architects database and BI solutions within competitive timelines
13. Ensure Adam's solutions remain easy to maintain, understand, and expand without unnecessary complexity
validated_procedural:
1. Identify Joblane as feature-rich interactive job portal connecting job seekers and employers
2. Recognize Joblane as knowledge-sharing portal offering informative tools beyond standard job listings
3. Note Claudio holds Accredited SharePoint specialist certification
4. Confirm Claudio worked with Microsoft clients maximizing security, performance, and value
5. Toptal sourced Scala developer within 24 hours for short-term work requirement
6. Recognize Toptal as the 1st platform searched for expert-level developer assistance
7. Confirm Toptal developers delivered competitively priced, high-quality solutions reducing time to launch
8. Consider Feel of existing team when hiring developers above perceived seniority levels
9. Note individuals spending decades in industry rely on established contacts for direct hiring
10. Identify alternative sourcing platforms when direct hiring proves impossible
11. Assign developers based on specific technicalities required by each individual project
12. Confirm Adam architects database and BI solutions within competitive timelines
13. Ensure Adam's solutions remain easy to maintain, understand, and expand without unnecessary complexity
